Motion For Relief Of Evidentiary Sanctions (9/16 Ost)
Off Calendar Oct-03-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. 415-434-1144 for Plaintiff.
John Bassett, Esq. 415-981-5451 Law office of Niven & Smith for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues and requests relief from evidentiary sanctions.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER FOR RELIEF FROM WAIVER [SIC] OF EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANTS' REQUESTS FOR WRITTEN DISCOVERY.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff's motion for an order relieving her from the evidentiary sanctions and waiver of her objections to discovery imposed by the court's April 9 and June 20, 2019 orders is denied.

This action was filed on September 24, 2018. On October 25, 2018, defendants served a request for production of documents on plaintiff. On November 9, plaintiff sought and was granted a 30-day extension of time to respond to that RFP, until December 30, 2018. However, plaintiff did not timely respond, and correspondence from defendants' counsel (dated January 10 and February 15, 2019) inquiring as to the status of plaintiff's discovery responses went unanswered.

(see word minutes for full details) (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Verified Response And Production Of Documents Propounded To Plaintiff Ashley Howitt
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Atty John W. Bassett of Niven & Smith LLP 415-981-5451 appeared for defendant. No other appearances.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendants' Motion to Compel Production of Documents from Plaintiff is unopposed and granted. Verified responses without objection, along with all documents responsive to defendants' Request for Production, Set One, shall be served within twenty days of service of a copy of the Order herein with Notice of Entry. Monetary sanctions in the sum of $1,185.95 are awarded against plaintiff to be paid within the aforesaid twenty days. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein, Clerk K.
